name: Cheso Aragonese (Hecho/Echo valley)

ancestors.notes: Cheso is a Western High-Aragonese valley variety inheriting the whole Aragonese grapheme→IPA system; it declares only the ⟨x⟩/⟨ix⟩ no-epenthesis delta and documents its lexical/morphological distinctiveness in notes.
Hispanic Vulgar Latin, inherited via Aragonese (an).
Gascon/Occitan Pyrenean adstrate, inherited via Aragonese.

notes: Cheso — the Aragonese of the Echo/Hecho valley (Valle de Hecho, Aragón Subordán, Huesca), the most prestigious and productive literary variety of Aragonese (Veremundo Méndez Coarasa's poetry). A Western High-Aragonese variety. PHONOLOGICALLY-EXPRESSED DELTA modelled here: (1) the prepalatal fricative ⟨x⟩/⟨ix⟩ = [ʃ] is realised WITHOUT the epenthetic/palatal-glide vowel found in eastern High-Aragonese varieties, so ⟨ix⟩ is set to [ʃ] alone (the base an lists [ʃ, iʃ, jʃ]) — the Western realisation described for the valley (es.wikipedia 'Aragonés cheso', secondary; ultimately after Kuhn 1935 and the Cheso literary tradition). Cheso otherwise keeps the full Western Aragonese profile inherited from an: F- preserved ([f]-, feito, fillo), /θ/ distinción, palatal lateral /ʎ/ for ⟨ll⟩ (no yeísmo), ⟨ch⟩ = [tʃ]. FEATURES NOT PHONEME-MAPPABLE (documented, not modelled, because Aragonese orthography is phonemic and these are lexical/etymological, not grapheme→IPA rules): loss of the affricate [tʃ] in a closed set of etyma from Latin initial J-/G- (itar < IECTARE, hermano < GERMANU — words simply spelled without ⟨ch⟩, so the base map already reads them correctly); word-final /r/ of the infinitive is PRESERVED even before enclitics (contrast Ansotano, where it is deleted) — a lexical/positional retention already yielded by the base r → [ɾ]. MORPHOSYNTAX (out of phoneme scope): article o/a/os/as; auxiliary 'ser' for intransitive-movement/reflexive/naixer·morir; past-participle agreement with pronominal direct objects. Diphthongisation of Latin Ĕ/Ŏ → ie/ue is the Alto-Aragonés norm here (Várvaro, AFA XLVI-XLVII, p. 250: 'ué no tiene competidores' in the Hecho documents). PROVENANCE HONESTY: per-valley phonetics drawn from the Spanish-Wikipedia dialect article (secondary) which cites Kuhn (1935) and Méndez Coarasa; the Várvaro (AFA) diphthongisation study was read directly; Kuhn's primary text was not obtainable.

sources.author: Kuhn, A.
sources.title: Der hocharagonesische Dialekt
sources.publisher: Revue de Linguistique Romane XI, pp. 1-312
sources.notes: The classic High-Aragonese dialect monograph, foundational for Ansó and Hecho. Cited via the secondary Spanish-Wikipedia dialect article; the primary text was not directly obtainable in this work.

sources.author: Várvaro, A.
sources.title: De la escritura al habla: la diptongación de O breve tónica en el Alto Aragón
sources.notes: Read directly. Documents the medieval geography of Ŏ-diphthongisation (ué/uá) across Hecho, Panticosa, Tena, Bielsa, Ansó and Gistaín (p. 250: Hecho shows only ué).
